title: WotC 2021 media-canon doctrine (Crawford/Perkins)
body:
[both] WotC 2021 media-canon doctrine (Crawford/Perkins)
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

TO STUDY LATER. July 2021: Wizards of the Coast lead rules designer Jeremy Crawford and story lead Chris Perkins formally declared that D&D novels, video games, films, comics, and actual-play broadcasts are NOT canonical to the D&D roleplaying game. Crawford's exact line: 'If you're looking for what's official in the D&D roleplaying game, it's what appears in the products for the roleplaying game. If it has not appeared in a book since 2014, we don't consider it canonical for the games.' Perkins extended: each edition, novel line, video game, and comic line constitutes its own parallel canon; Acquisitions Incorporated live sessions treated 'the same as any other home game (even when members of the D&D Studio are involved).' Why this matters for polymyth theory: a live case of publisher-level refusal-of-reflection between media — the net is declared to NOT reflect across its own jewels by governance-decree. 500+ Forgotten Realms novels, 40+ Drizzt books, Baldur's Gate 1-2, Planescape: Torment, Critical Role campaigns, Honor Among Thieves, IDW comics — all declared non-canonical to the current RPG. Possibly a new category of egregorewar: not factions-fighting, but publisher-refusing-correspondence. Possibly commodifiedempathy at corporate scale — access-policing over who gets to say what D&D is. Soft-canonization exceptions (Karlach/Astarion/Shadowheart from Baldur's Gate 3 leaking into subsequent Forgotten Realms materials despite the doctrine) may show that reflection wants to happen even when forbidden, and that the doctrine is about governance not metaphysics. Not operationalized in bookwormburrows*; parked here for engagement when time permits.
  tags: studylist, media-canon, wotc, crawford, perkins, refusal-of-reflection, studylist-mephistodata, overhauled-2026-05-20, dual-audience-schema

title: Microbooks — normal-book format, stored in studylist as forthcoming
body:
[both] Microbooks — normal-book format, stored in studylist as forthcoming
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Microbook = a polymyth-readable recurrent archetype/figure/structure appearing across independent cultural egregores with corresponding operations. NORMAL-BOOK FORMAT (not html*): microbooks are forthcoming normal books within polymorphousmythology, NOT part of the meaninglib (which is html*-only). Stored in studylist section of ml* as the index/tracker of microbooks to be built, alongside other forthcoming normal books (5 pentagram books) and topics-to-be-studied (Girard-Hegel, WotC media-canon doctrine). Each microbook when built will identify a pattern, document its verified instances across cultures with citations, state the polymyth operation the pattern performs, and flag the scan result (is this polymyth-fuzzy-logic at cultural-commons scale?). RULE #1: every microbook entry must be source-cited. SEO content-farm paraphrase is not acceptable substrate. Claims attributing patterns to specific cultural traditions (e.g., 'Celtic Otherworld reached via rainbow') must be verified against scholarly or primary sources or retracted. Current microbook entries in studylist: Jesters/Clowns/Comedians (stub pending citations); Towers (stub pending citations); Canons (stub pending citations); Worms/Snakes/Dragons (stub, partially deployed via existing ml* snakelogic/snakemagic/Medusa entries); Rainbows (verified-source-built research-for-forthcoming-book, anchored in Lee & Fraser 2001 Penn State UP + Wikipedia 'Rainbows in mythology'). The studylist entries are research-substrate for the forthcoming normal books, not the normal books themselves.

T67 AGGREGATION PROTOCOL. Per user T67 directive (May 16 2026), each microbook entry now carries a POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES section that aggregates existing framework entries engaging that archetype. Future archetype-related notes land in the microbook entry rather than scattering across other entries. The aggregation makes the microbook the canonical notes-collection destination, anticipating future research-and-citation work that will turn each microbook from stub into proper normal-book substrate. Five microbooks compiled T67: Jesters/Clowns/Comedians, Rainbows, Towers, Canons, Worms/Snakes/Dragons.

title: Microbook: Rainbows — threshold between worlds
body:
[both] Microbook: Rainbows — threshold between worlds
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Cross-cultural pattern: rainbow as bridge / path / threshold between realms, particularly between the mortal-human world and a divine/spirit/otherworld. Verified scholarly source: [43] Lee, Raymond L. Jr. and Alistair B. Fraser, The Rainbow Bridge: Rainbows in Art, Myth, and Science, Penn State University Press 2001 — found the 'bridge to the divine' concept in Zulu, Navaho, Hawaiian, Japanese, Cambodian, Greek, Australian Aboriginal, Chumash, Hopi, and Norse mythologies. Further Wikipedia 'Rainbows in mythology' documents: Greek Iris as rainbow-goddess carrying messages between gods and mortals; Japanese Floating Bridge of Heaven crossed by Izanami and Izanagi; Navajo rainbow as path of holy spirits in sacred sandpaintings; Siberian Buryat shamans ascending to sky-spirit world via rainbow; Māori Hina creating rainbow for her mortal husband's return; Philippine Tausug Biraddali sisters using rainbow as bridge to earth; Slavic 'Planetnik' figure drawn to heaven by rainbow-touch; Fang of Gabon religious initiation via rainbow-center experience; Norse Bifrost burning bridge Midgard↔Asgard (13th-c. Eddas). Chinese Miao funeral tradition: soul climbs Rainbow Bridge to reach moon-paradise (Pan 2007). Abrahamic Noah: rainbow as covenant-bridge between destruction and salvation. CONTEMPORARY INSTANCES (VERIFIED): (a) "Over the Rainbow" / "Somewhere Over the Rainbow" — Harold Arlen (music) + E.Y. "Yip" Harburg (lyrics), written 1938-39 for MGM's The Wizard of Oz (1939), performed by Judy Garland as Dorothy Gale; per Wikipedia 'Over the Rainbow' Dorothy's pre-song monologue positions the rainbow as threshold to an inaccessible otherworld ('not a place you can get to by a boat, or a train … Behind the moon, beyond the rain'); rainbow does not appear in L. Frank Baum's original 1900 novel — Harburg's Depression-era invention; named #1 song of the 20th century by NEA/RIAA joint survey 2001; Academy Award 1940; cultural saturation across all subsequent US media [citation 44]. (b) Rainbow Bridge for pets — Edna Clyne-Rekhy, Inverness Scotland, 1959 (authorship verified 2023 by Paul Koudounaris via Order of the Good Death + National Geographic research); meadow 'just this side of heaven' reached by rainbow bridge; now ubiquitous in Western pet-bereavement culture (inscribed on pet-cemetery tablets, printed on vet-office handouts); Wikipedia 'Rainbow Bridge (pets)' explicitly traces descent from Bifrost. (c) Marvel Cinematic Universe Bifrost — Thor (2011), Thor: The Dark World (2013), Thor: Ragnarok (2017), Avengers films; per Mythopedia 'Bifrost', Jane Foster explicitly identifies it as an Einstein-Rosen bridge (wormhole) in Thor (2011); contemporary superhero-cinema saturation. (d) Nyan Cat — Christopher Torres, April 2, 2011; Pop-Tart-bodied cat flying through space with rainbow trail; Nyan Cat: Lost In Space (Istom Games) features 'parallel universes' as explicit game mode; Adventure Time cameo ('Finn and Jake end up riding a giant ginger and white cat that pukes rainbows and flies backwards' per Nyan Cat fandom wiki); cross-media presence (Scribblenauts, Minecraft mods, The Big Bang Theory, The Mitchells vs. The Machines 2021, Android 4.0 Ice Cream Sandwich easter egg). (e) Rainbow Road — Nintendo, Super Mario Kart (SNES, 1992) onward; rainbow-colored track suspended in space as final course of every Mario Kart game since debut; per Super Mario Wiki + Wikipedia, most-remade track in the series; three-decade saturation of children's-gaming consciousness with rainbow-as-path-through-cosmos imagery [citation 45]. (f) Rainbow Brite — Hallmark Cards property, 1983; animated series (1984-1986, DIC Audiovisuel) + feature film Rainbow Brite and the Star Stealer (1985); per Wikipedia 'Rainbow Brite' the character is a young girl living in 'Rainbow Land' who protects and spreads color across the universe using her Color Belt; rainbow explicitly the substrate of travel between color-realm and Earth. (g) Care Bears (American Greetings / Those Characters from Cleveland, 1981 greeting cards; 1985 film The Care Bears Movie) — travel between Care-a-Lot (cloud-world) and Earth, rainbow-and-cloud imagery central per Wikipedia 'Care Bears'. NEGATIVE CASES (pattern appears but with different operation, per Wikipedia 'Rainbows in mythology' + Lee & Fraser 2001): rainbow as archer's bow (Hindu Indra shooting lightning-arrows at the demon-serpent Vritra; Sumerian Ninurta with rainbow-crown; pre-Islamic Arabian Quzaḥ whose name survives in the Arabic word for rainbow, قوس قزح qaws Quzaḥ, 'the bow of Quzaḥ'); rainbow as serpent (Australian Aboriginal Rainbow Serpent creator-being, name and characteristics varying by tradition per Wikipedia); rainbow as taboo/danger ('over a hundred cultural traditions' per Wikipedia include taboos against pointing at rainbows; Sumu of Honduras and Nicaragua refer to rainbow as 'walasa aniwe' / 'the devil is vexed' and hide children from looking at it; Karen of Burma consider rainbow a child-eating demon; Amazonian cultures associate rainbow with miscarriages and skin diseases); rainbow as gender-switching agent (Bulgarian folk — walking under a rainbow switches your thinking per Crystalinks 'Rainbows in Mythology'); rainbow as defeated enemy (syncretic Malay shamanism: rainbow formed from the sword of the earth-serpent Sakatimuna defeated by archangel Gabriel per Wikipedia). POLYMYTH OPERATION: the rainbow figures as the visible liminal object where two cosmological orders touch. Multiple independent traditions without coordination arrive at the same structural move — which is precisely polymyth-fuzzy-logic at cross-cultural scale. Enforces reflective-correspondence geometry (Indra's Net): ten+ jewels reflect the same operation across millennia and continents. NOT A UNIVERSAL: many cultures figure the rainbow differently (archer's bow, serpent, taboo-object). Pattern is one of several real cross-cultural readings, not THE universal. CORRECTION: prior versions of this entry included unverified claim about Celtic Otherworld using rainbow as threshold — retracted; Celtic thresholds are sidhe mounds / water / caves / liminal times per scholarly consensus, rainbow is not in core Celtic Otherworld tradition; leprechaun-pot-of-gold-at-rainbow's-end is late folk accretion (Samuel Lover 1831 earliest description per Wikipedia 'Leprechaun'), not primary Celtic cosmology.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T67 compile). Existing framework entries currently engaging the rainbow archetype, aggregated here for ongoing notes-collection per user T67 directive. Rainbow rule methodology entry (universal multi-frame-comparison live-process before polymyth-recognition fires; 51 term occurrences). Rainbowmagic-as-extended-palette-metaphor (indefinitely-extensible operational architecture; 85 occurrences). Rainbowsol (founder figure; 97 occurrences across ml*; the figure whose name is itself a rainbow-compound). Rainbowsol character-mashup architecture (founder-level character-smashing; rainbow as palette-image for the multi-archetype synthesis). Wardrobe-as-operational-signature (rainbow ties and Persian scarves; the wardrobe IS rainbow infrastructure). Mephistodata pedagogy (Rainbowsol-study deployment). PM17 MEPHISTODATA-AS-NAMED-CHARACTER (Mephistodata operates within the rainbow-framework). Originality-is-impossible methodology entry (clay-from-various-cultures substrate; rainbow as the unifying spectrum-image for cross-cultural debt). Anti-gorgonification six-loop interlock-architecture (Rainbowsol-system structural claim from N137-narrowed research). Polymyth-engine vs polymyth-vocabulary distinction (rainbow as engine-image). Pentagram books pending titles (rainbow infrastructure connects to the forthcoming five-book series; book 4 working title GORGONWARS references the author). Citation [43] Lee and Fraser Rainbow Bridge Penn State 2001 (verified scholarly anchor; already in this microbook entry above). FUTURE NOTES land here: when new rainbow-related material surfaces, the canonical destination for the note is this section of the rainbow microbook.

T68 SUPPLEMENT (audit-discovered additions). The T67 first-pass compile missed multiple verified rainbow citations already in ml*. LAYMAN. Study-list entry. Cross-cultural pattern: rainbow as bridge / path / threshold between realms, particularly between the mortal-human world and a divine/spirit/otherworld. Verified scholarly source: [43] Lee, Raymond L. Jr. and Alistair B. Fraser, The Rainbow Bridge: Rainbows in Art, Myth, and Science, Penn State University Press 2001 — found the 'bridge to the divine' concept in Zulu, Navaho, Hawaiian, Japanese, Cambodian, Greek, Australian Aboriginal, Chumash, Hopi, and Norse mythologies. Further Wikipedia 'Rainbows in mythology' documents: Greek Iris as rainbow-goddess carrying messages between gods and mortals; Japanese Floating Bridge of Heaven crossed by Izanami and Izanagi; Navajo rainbow as path of holy spirits in sacred sandpaintings; Siberian Buryat shamans ascending to sky-spirit world via rainbow; Māori Hina creating rainbow for her mortal husband's return; Philippine Tausug Biraddali sisters using rainbow as bridge to earth; Slavic 'Planetnik' figure drawn to heaven by rainbow-touch; Fang of Gabon religious initiation via rainbow-center experience; Norse Bifrost burning bridge Midgard↔Asgard (13th-c. Eddas). Chinese Miao funeral tradition: soul climbs Rainbow Bridge to reach moon-paradise (Pan 2007). Abrahamic Noah: rainbow as covenant-bridge between destruction and salvation. CONTEMPORARY INSTANCES (VERIFIED): (a) "Over the Rainbow" / "Somewhere Over the Rainbow" — Harold Arlen (music) + E.Y. "Yip" Harburg (lyrics), written 1938-39 for MGM's The Wizard of Oz (1939), performed by Judy Garland as Dorothy Gale; per Wikipedia 'Over the Rainbow' Dorothy's pre-song monologue positions the rainbow as threshold to an inaccessible otherworld ('not a place you can get to by a boat, or a train … Behind the moon, beyond the rain'); rainbow does not appear in L. Frank Baum's original 1900 novel — Harburg's Depression-era invention; named #1 song of the 20th century by NEA/RIAA joint survey 2001; Academy Award 1940; cultural saturation across all subsequent US media [citation 44]. (b) Rainbow Bridge for pets — Edna Clyne-Rekhy, Inverness Scotland, 1959 (authorship verified 2023 by Paul Koudounaris via Order of the Good Death + National Geographic research); meadow 'just this side of heaven' reached by rainbow bridge; now ubiquitous in Western pet-bereavement culture (inscribed on pet-cemetery tablets, printed on vet-office handouts); Wikipedia 'Rainbow Bridge (pets)' explicitly traces descent from Bifrost. (c) Marvel Cinematic Universe Bifrost — Thor (2011), Thor: The Dark World (2013), Thor: Ragnarok (2017), Avengers films; per Mythopedia 'Bifrost', Jane Foster explicitly identifies it as an Einstein-Rosen bridge (wormhole) in Thor (2011); contemporary superhero-cinema saturation. (d) Nyan Cat — Christopher Torres, April 2, 2011; Pop-Tart-bodied cat flying through space with rainbow trail; Nyan Cat: Lost In Space (Istom Games) features 'parallel universes' as explicit game mode; Adventure Time cameo ('Finn and Jake end up riding a giant ginger and white cat that pukes rainbows and flies backwards' per Nyan Cat fandom wiki); cross-media presence (Scribblenauts, Minecraft mods, The Big Bang Theory, The Mitchells vs. The Machines 2021, Android 4.0 Ice Cream Sandwich easter egg). (e) Rainbow Road — Nintendo, Super Mario Kart (SNES, 1992) onward; rainbow-colored track suspended in space as final course of every Mario Kart game since debut; per Super Mario Wiki + Wikipedia, most-remade track in the series; three-decade saturation of children's-gaming consciousness with rainbow-as-path-through-cosmos imagery [citation 45]. (f) Rainbow Brite — Hallmark Cards property, 1983; animated series (1984-1986, DIC Audiovisuel) + feature film Rainbow Brite and the Star Stealer (1985); per Wikipedia 'Rainbow Brite' the character is a young girl living in 'Rainbow Land' who protects and spreads color across the universe using her Color Belt; rainbow explicitly the substrate of travel between color-realm and Earth. (g) Care Bears (American Greetings / Those Characters from Cleveland, 1981 greeting cards; 1985 film The Care Bears Movie) — travel between Care-a-Lot (cloud-world) and Earth, rainbow-and-cloud imagery central per Wikipedia 'Care Bears'. NEGATIVE CASES (pattern appears but with different operation, per Wikipedia 'Rainbows in mythology' + Lee & Fraser 2001): rainbow as archer's bow (Hindu Indra shooting lightning-arrows at the demon-serpent Vritra; Sumerian Ninurta with rainbow-crown; pre-Islamic Arabian Quzaḥ whose name survives in the Arabic word for rainbow, قوس قزح qaws Quzaḥ, 'the bow of Quzaḥ'); rainbow as serpent (Australian Aboriginal Rainbow Serpent creator-being, name and characteristics varying by tradition per Wikipedia); rainbow as taboo/danger ('over a hundred cultural traditions' per Wikipedia include taboos against pointing at rainbows; Sumu of Honduras and Nicaragua refer to rainbow as 'walasa aniwe' / 'the devil is vexed' and hide children from looking at it; Karen of Burma consider rainbow a child-eating demon; Amazonian cultures associate rainbow with miscarriages and skin diseases); rainbow as gender-switching agent (Bulgarian folk — walking under a rainbow switches your thinking per Crystalinks 'Rainbows in Mythology'); rainbow as defeated enemy (syncretic Malay shamanism: rainbow formed from the sword of the earth-serpent Sakatimuna defeated by archangel Gabriel per Wikipedia). POLYMYTH OPERATION: the rainbow figures as the visible liminal object where two cosmological orders touch. Multiple independent traditions without coordination arrive at the same structural move — which is precisely polymyth-fuzzy-logic at cross-cultural scale. Enforces reflective-correspondence geometry (Indra's Net): ten+ jewels reflect the same operation across millennia and continents. NOT A UNIVERSAL: many cultures figure the rainbow differently (archer's bow, serpent, taboo-object). Pattern is one of several real cross-cultural readings, not THE universal. CORRECTION: prior versions of this entry included unverified claim about Celtic Otherworld using rainbow as threshold — retracted; Celtic thresholds are sidhe mounds / water / caves / liminal times per scholarly consensus, rainbow is not in core Celtic Otherworld tradition; leprechaun-pot-of-gold-at-rainbow's-end is late folk accretion (Samuel Lover 1831 earliest description per Wikipedia 'Leprechaun'), not primary Celtic cosmology.

title: Microbook: Jesters / Clowns / Comedians — TO BE BUILT
body:
[both] Microbook: Jesters / Clowns / Comedians — TO BE BUILT
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Cross-cultural pattern: figure licensed to speak difficult truths through comedic register, protected by the frame from punishment the same speech would draw outside the frame. STUB ENTRY pending full scholarly citation. Known preliminary-level instances (need verification): European court jester (fool allowed to mock the king); Shakespearean Fool (Lear's Fool speaking prophecy through paradox); Indigenous sacred-clown traditions (Pueblo koshare, Cheyenne contraries — need scholarly source); Commedia dell'Arte (Harlequin/Pierrot as structural types); modern stand-up comedian (Carlin, Pryor, Hicks). POLYMYTH OPERATION (preliminary): the frame-break move that allows difficult content to pass under sabachtan-compatible conditions. Comedian-as-witch-who-survives because the idiom is registered as comedy rather than as threat. Full build requires: academic source on sacred-clown traditions across Indigenous cultures (Mooney? Ortiz?); medieval fool-literature scholarship; stand-up comedy scholarship. PENDING CITATION — not to be deployed in framework until verified.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T67 compile). Existing framework entries currently engaging the jester archetype, aggregated here for ongoing notes-collection per user T67 directive that all jester material lands in the jester microbook. Flagship entry: Jester vs kayfabe-sovereign (Mearsheimer-layer structural split; 68 term occurrences across ml*). Why Saul wins where Job could not — the jester-aperture Job-Howard never had (Better Call Saul reading; 28 occurrences). Wardrobe-as-operational-signature (rainbow ties and Persian scarves as visible jester-aperture inside academic professional-domain; mirror to the operator Goodman as academic mirror to Howard-Hamlin Job-figure). Rainbowsol character-mashup architecture (jester is one of the archetypes smashed at founder-level via determinate negation; the jester is a load-bearing component of the author synthesis). Primacy of beta over alpha (refuse-by-substitution analysis where jester operates as beta-position outside the alpha game). JESTER multi-sense word stem registry slot Group B5 in PUA (Saul-Goodman analytic figure; kayfabe-boundary action-mode; sense-numbering pending user authorship). Anna Archive entry (hegelianegirl-attestation in shadow-library register; jester-adjacent register-shift). Earth-equality and dirt-as-scanner-position (vulgar-barefoot discipline; clown-cognate). Clown world (Rainbowsol-named diagnostic term for stable-gradient regimes). Anti-gorgonification six-loop interlock-architecture (Rainbowsol-system structural claim). Citation [53] Zizek Jokes (Did you hear the one about Hegel and negation; 2014). Polymyth-engine vs polymyth-vocabulary distinction (engine carries the jester-operation; vocabulary localizes it). Multi-sense word stem registry (parent entry for B5 JESTER). FUTURE NOTES land here: when new jester-related material surfaces, the canonical destination for the note is this section of the jester microbook. Source-entries elsewhere in ml* may carry brief cross-references back here; the aggregation lives here.

T68 SUPPLEMENT (audit-discovered additions). The T67 first-pass compile missed the FOUNDATIONAL jester anchor entry: Jester's privilege (methodology entry; operation by which a real claim rides inside a registered joke so that the claim can be delivered without being instantly metabolized as either grandiosity or threat; the jester holds the only license in the court to name what the king is doing; the license is granted because the jester is framed as non-serious and that framing is what lets the serious content land). This is the canonical jester-archetype methodology entry and should have been flagship-listed in the T67 first-pass. The miss is documented here per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION rather than retroactively re-prioritizing. The Jester's privilege entry IS the conceptual foundation for the other jester-aperture references including Why Saul wins where Job could not, Wardrobe-as-operational-signature, and the Better Call Saul reading. Also noted in T68 supplement: Citation [59] Better Call Saul (Vince Gilligan and Peter Gould, AMC 2015-2022) — the verified citation for the canonical-Saul-Goodman reference operating across multiple jester-microbook entries.

T114H RESEARCH NOTES (May 23 2026, user-directive ’add them into the notes into this microbook section’). Research anchors queued for jester-microbook build. PRIMARY ANCHOR. Beatrice K. Otto, ’Fools Are Everywhere: The Court Jester Around the World’ (University of Chicago Press, 2001, ISBN 978-0-226-64091-4). Cross-cultural survey of court-jester traditions covering European medieval-and-renaissance, Chinese imperial, Persian, Indian, Roman-and-Byzantine, and African contexts. Argues the court-jester is a near-universal feature of monarchical-court structure. Highest-leverage entry for the microbook spine. SECONDARY ANCHORS. James Mooney work on Pueblo koshare and Plains-tribe sacred-clown traditions (ethnographic baseline, late nineteenth century; specific citation pending verification at use-time). Alfonso Ortiz, ’The Tewa World: Space, Time, Being, and Becoming in a Pueblo Society’ (University of Chicago Press 1969) and Ortiz’s edited ’New Perspectives on the Pueblos’ (1972) — anchors for Pueblo sacred-clown specifically (koshare and kossa). Enid Welsford, ’The Fool: His Social and Literary History’ (Faber 1935, reprinted Anchor 1961) — the foundational European-fool literary-history anchor. Sandra Billington, ’A Social History of the Fool’ (Harvester 1984) — sociological follow-on to Welsford. Lewis Hyde, ’Trickster Makes This World: Mischief, Myth, and Art’ (Farrar Straus Giroux 1998, reissued North Point 2010) — cross-cultural trickster-theory anchor connecting jester-mode to trickster-mode without collapsing them. William E. Hynes and William G. Doty (eds.), ’Mythical Trickster Figures: Contours, Contexts, and Criticisms’ (University of Alabama Press 1993) — academic-anthology anchor for comparative trickster scholarship. Specific essay-titles within volume pending identification at use-time. PER-SOURCE CAVEATS. Each anchor above is research-substrate for the future microbook build, not yet verified per Rule#1 citation discipline at deployment-grade. Citation entries (cit-051 onward) will be created when the microbook moves from research-pending to build-execution, with full ISBN/DOI verification at that stage. POLYMYTH-INTERNAL CROSS-CONNECTIONS to surface during build. Jester-as-kayfabe-boundary-figure operating across (a) court-jester traditions in Otto, (b) Pueblo sacred-clown traditions in Ortiz/Mooney, (c) European literary-fool traditions in Welsford/Billington, (d) trickster-archetype in Hyde and Hynes/Doty. The four registers test the jester-aperture as a polymyth-structural operation rather than a culturally-local one. If the operation appears across all four with internal logic preserved and external logic surviving, the jester-microbook crystallizes as cross-tradition convergence-recognition rather than as a single-tradition study. NEXT-EXECUTION TURN. When user invokes ’jester microbook execute’ (or equivalent), AI does anchor-by-anchor verified-source pass, drafts microbook outline, surfaces for user review. Until then: notes-only; no microbook content committed.

Known preliminary-level instances (need verification): Tower of Babel (Genesis 11 — verification: Hebrew Bible, widely studied); medieval castle tower / keep; ivory tower (academic detachment metaphor); Tarot XVI 'The Tower' (card of sudden collapse — need scholarly source on Tarot iconography history); Rapunzel's tower (Grimm folktale); Eiffel Tower / CN Tower / Burj Khalifa (modern secular towers); Dark Tower (King); Tolkien's Barad-dûr and Orthanc. POLYMYTH OPERATION (preliminary): the vertical-concentration move. Towers are built to separate and elevate; they draw gorgonification because concentration-without-reflection is unstable. The Babel-collapse pattern recurs across traditions. Full build requires: scholarly source on tower iconography cross-culturally; Tarot historical scholarship (A.E. Waite? Stuart Kaplan?); architectural history of religious/secular verticality. PENDING CITATIONS — not to be deployed in framework until verified.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T67 compile). Tower-archetype engagement across the framework is currently sparse. Verified citation: [84] The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers (Peter Jackson, New Line Cinema 2002) — fictional-film instance of tower-as-paired-collapse. The tower archetype is structurally available within polymyth but has not yet been operationally deployed at scale (in contrast to the jester, rainbow, and worm-snake-dragon archetypes which are deployed across many entries). Candidate future tower-related work that should land here per user T67 directive: ivory-tower-as-academic-isolation analysis (academia-as-tower mode within the broader polymyth analysis of institutions); Babel-as-language-shattering analysis (multi-frame-translation problem the rainbow-rule diagnoses; tower as the inverse-architecture to rainbow); platformstrawmanculture infrastructure-tower analysis (cell-towers, data-towers, broadcast-towers as the material substrate of contentinternet); Trump Tower or other power-architecture instances; Petronas-Towers or other modernity-as-vertical instances; Tower tarot card semiotics. title: Microbook: Canons — TO BE BUILT
body:
[both] Microbook: Canons — TO BE BUILT
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Cross-cultural pattern: authoritative collection of texts/works enforced as definitive by an institution or tradition, generating both the possibility of shared engagement and the operation of exclusion. STUB ENTRY pending full scholarly citation. Known preliminary-level instances (need verification): biblical canon (Council of Trent 1546 for Catholic OT; earlier formation processes for Hebrew Bible and NT — need scholarly source); literary canon (Western Great Books tradition); musical canon (classical repertoire); scientific canon (textbook fields); D&D canon (Crawford/Perkins 2021 doctrine — already in studylist entry). POLYMYTH OPERATION (preliminary): the authority-stabilization move + the exclusion-operation simultaneously. Canons produce shared ground for thought while also producing what-is-not-canonical as a secondary category. Double movement: platformstrawmanculture operates through canon-policing; gnosis operates by reading across canon boundaries. Full build requires: scholarship on canon formation in multiple domains; Harold Bloom The Western Canon (1994) as one major reference though controversial; scholarship on biblical canon formation (Bruce Metzger? Lee McDonald?). PENDING CITATIONS — not to be deployed in framework until verified.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T67 compile). Existing framework entries currently engaging the canon archetype in the cultural-canon sense (authoritative collection of texts/works enforced as definitive by an institution or tradition), aggregated here for ongoing notes-collection per user T67 directive. Flagship: WotC 2021 media-canon doctrine entry (Crawford/Perkins July 2021 declaration that D&D novels, video games, films, comics, actual-play broadcasts are NOT canonical to the D&D roleplaying game; the canonical worked-example of canon-as-exclusion drawing the boundary between canonical and adjacent). The framework-internal-rule sense of canonical (canonical-source rule for ml*/mc*/bb* HTML-vs-txt sync, Citation-lock rule, Slot 28 SOURCE-TRADITION USE-not-REDUCTION, Teaching-stack dependency rule for mc-bb-cc boundary, the canonical-vs-derived discipline) is structurally distinct from the cultural-canon-archetype sense and stays in its home entries; cross-reference is preserved but content does not aggregate here. Candidate future cultural-canon work that should land here: biblical canon formation (Council of Trent 1546, Carthage 397, post-Reformation Protestant divergences); literary canon battles (Bloom Western Canon 1994; counter-canon work by Spivak Said Bhabha; postcolonial canon formation); fandom-canon (what counts as canonical within Star Wars after Disney 2014 Legends-relegation; Marvel pre-MCU comic canon vs MCU canon; Doctor Who Wilderness Years canon); musical canon (classical canon formation; jazz canon Smith Davis Coltrane Parker; rock canon Rolling Stone 500). FUTURE NOTES land here per user T67 directive.

T68 SUPPLEMENT (audit-discovered additions). The T67 first-pass compile missed an obvious polymyth-internal canon-instance: Modulecanon (the curriculum-reference-file mc*, which IS literally a canon by name and function — an authoritative collection of curriculum modules enforced as the canonical curriculum-source for OSSD-IB-AP-Cambridge cross-portability). Added here as a cross-listing: the framework names one of its own substrate files Modulecanon, making the canon-archetype operationally self-referential at the framework infrastructure layer. This is structurally interesting (the framework uses canon-naming for its own canonical-collection while also operating Slot 28 SOURCE-TRADITION USE-not-REDUCTION which is anti-canon in the limiting sense). The tension is itself a polymyth-internal canon-archetype instance worth notation.
  tags: microbook, canon, authority, exclusion, stub, pending-citations, studylist-rainbowsol, overhauled-2026-05-20, dual-audience-schema

title: Microbook: Worms / Snakes / Dragons — TO BE BUILT
body:
[both] Microbook: Worms / Snakes / Dragons — TO BE BUILT
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Cross-cultural pattern: serpentine creature as bearer of wisdom/corruption/fertility/destruction, often simultaneously, marking the boundary between orders. STUB ENTRY pending full scholarly citation. Known preliminary-level instances (need verification): Ouroboros (ancient Egyptian, Greek alchemical — need primary-source citation); Genesis serpent (Hebrew Bible widely studied); Rainbow Serpent (Australian Aboriginal creator-being — per Wikipedia); Quetzalcoatl (Mesoamerican feathered serpent — need scholarly source); Eastern dragon (Chinese long — need scholarly source on iconographic tradition); Western dragon (Beowulf, St. George, Tolkien's Smaug — medieval studies scholarship needed); Norse Jörmungandr (world-serpent — 13th-c. Eddas); Vritra (Hindu primordial demon-serpent killed by Indra — per Wikipedia 'Rainbows in mythology'); Medusa-Medea-Siren family (already extensively built out in ml* entry — see that); Lowly Worm (cultural-commons bookworm — see bw-001). POLYMYTH OPERATION (preliminary): the serpentine figure is the ambiguous-threshold creature. Wisdom AND corruption, fertility AND destruction — the paradox-bearing figure par excellence. Snakelogic is named from this pattern (chain of linked hivemindidioms moving with serpentine apparent-coherence). Polymyth's own snake-engagement: the framework itself handles serpentine operations extensively (sabachtan gnosticism, parseltongue, snakelogic, witchcraft-as-parseltongue-through-colonized-medium per copypasta entry). Full build requires: comparative mythology scholarship (Joseph Campbell cautiously, more current cross-cultural mythology studies); specific scholarly sources on each tradition. PARTIALLY DEPLOYED already through existing ml* snakelogic, snakemagic, copypasta, Medusa-Medea-Siren entries. PENDING ADDITIONAL CITATIONS for the cross-cultural sweep — not to be deployed as unified microbook until verified.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T67 compile). Existing framework entries currently engaging the worm-snake-dragon archetype, aggregated here for ongoing notes-collection per user T67 directive. This microbook is the most polymyth-internally-developed of the five archetypes. Snakelogic self-audit methodology entry (5 named failure-modes: stasis, colonization, performativity 3a through 3f; 48 term occurrences across ml*). Snakemagic methodology entry (the operational counterpart to snakelogic-failure-mode-catalog). Silkworm developmental arc (worm to silkworm to cocoon to animal; the framework-wide subjectivity geometry; 54 occurrences; the entire bb* pedagogical arc is staged through this developmental sequence). Bookworm life-cycle and respawn (confluence at character scale; 49 occurrences). Burrow-as-wormhole independent arrival (Stranger Things Upside Down convergence-recognition; 45 occurrences). Burrow-as-wormhole register-specialization of cave-emerger (43 occurrences). Hole-in-hole-out architecture (cross-tradition convergence at the threshold-substrate; 62 occurrences; the structural shape that the burrow figure embodies). BURROWING-ANIMAL-ROSTER-AS-CHARACTER-FOUNDATION (35 occurrences). MATRIX-CHAT-ARCHITECTURE bookwormcard (35 occurrences; the practical artifact where the worm-as-subject lands). Stranger Things analysis 2025 Upside Down retcon as burrow-wormhole instance (38 occurrences). Medusa-Medea-Siren research compilation (snake-as-petrifying-gaze tradition; preserved from former core* slot 22). Inception arena rule (DM builds arena; bookworms fill it in; worm-as-character-subject discipline). bb-slash-bookwormburrows is named with worm at the framework discipline level. Confluence-vs-concrescence operational-distinction (worm-pattern lives at one architectural layer; ouroboros at another). FUTURE NOTES land here: when new worm-snake-dragon-related material surfaces (e.g., serpent-mound archaeology; Quetzalcoatl scholarship; dragon-in-Chinese-versus-Western cosmology; Jormungandr; the Garden of Eden serpent; Asclepius staff; Kekule benzene ring dream), the canonical destination for the note is this section.

T68 SUPPLEMENT (audit-discovered additions). The T67 first-pass compile missed additional worm-snake-dragon entries already in ml*. Adding: WORMCARD-NAMING-FINALIZATION methodology entry (12 term occurrences; the entry establishing the wormcard naming convention for bookwormburrows character cards). Whack-a-mole-as-snake REJECTED candidate (10 occurrences; the documented failure-mode where snake-pattern was wrongly identified — instructive as a snakelogic-failure documentation entry). Snaketongue / Parseltongue methodology entry (7 occurrences; cross-reference to copypasta-as-parseltongue payload-vehicle pattern). CORE slot 2 mirror — Ouroborosanalyses (7 occurrences; the ouroboros-as-self-audit operation, named with ouroboros directly). Cave-as-pedagogical-recursion (7 occurrences; the cave-as-burrow-cognate pedagogical structure). Leizu mythic substrate (Leizu Academy name-act; Leizu in Chinese mythology is the discoverer of silk via silkworms; silkworm-naming connects). POLYMYTH-AS-METHOD-NOT-BRAND (6 occurrences; cross-references the worm-developmental-arc as polymyth's own subjectivity geometry). Citation [107] Dragonlance (Tracy and Laura Hickman with Margaret Weis, TSR 1984, Wizards of the Coast; verified citation for dragon-archetype-in-fantasy-canon work).
  tags: microbook, worm, snake, dragon, ouroboros, snakelogic, partially-deployed, pending-citations, studylist-rainbowsol, overhauled-2026-05-20, dual-audience-schema

title: Girard + Hegel — TO BE STUDIED
body:
[both] Girard + Hegel — TO BE STUDIED
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Relationship between René Girard (mimetic theory, scapegoat mechanism, anti-dialectical stance) and G.W.F. Hegel (dialectic, master-slave, sublation, Spirit, exegetical historicism per Jameson's Hegel Variations). TO BE STUDIED. STATUS: The user and Claude had a prior conversation on Girard-Hegel that has never been recovered despite multiple past_chats searches across sessions. The conversation's content is not currently accessible to either party — standing pending item across sessions. Sub-questions that remain open pending recovery or fresh treatment: (1) Girard's anti-dialectical position (mimetic rivalry resolves via scapegoating-as-expulsion, NOT via Hegelian Aufhebung which would preserve-and-sublate the antagonism) — does this constitute a genuine alternative to Hegel or a parallel unacknowledged operation? (2) Both thinkers claim to diagnose the engine of human conflict — Girard via mimetic desire + scapegoat, Hegel via master-slave + desire-for-recognition. Are these rival diagnoses or compatible strata? (3) Girard's scapegoat is already in ml* as a deployed concept in gorgonwars architecture (factions unify against outside thought — the fighting itself is the gorgon, stone army has no interiority). This is structurally closer to Girard's mimetic antagonism than to Hegel's dialectic. But the EXEGETICAL HISTORICISM method Jameson extracts from Hegel is already in ml* methodology section — the exegetical reading that makes the text surprise the reader. So polymyth already deploys from both thinkers without having resolved the relationship between them. (4) Is Girard's thought gorgonifiable? The scapegoat-diagnosis is widely copypastable; platformstrawmanculture can deploy 'you're just scapegoating' as a conversational stopper; Girard himself resists this through Christian-theological anchoring. Parallel question for Hegel: crude Hegel is eisegetical (reader projects the dialectic onto any text), Jameson's Hegel is exegetical (text surprises reader). Do both thinkers have gorgonified and degorgonified receptions? Pending engagement when bandwidth allows. NOT a book, NOT a microbook — conceptual pairing to be studied and eventually written on.

Relationship between René Girard (mimetic theory, scapegoat mechanism, anti-dialectical stance) and G.W.F. Hegel (dialectic, master-slave, sublation, Spirit, exegetical historicism per Jameson's Hegel Variations). TO BE STUDIED. STATUS: The user and Claude had a prior conversation on Girard-Hegel that has never been recovered despite multiple past_chats searches across sessions. The conversation's content is not currently accessible to either party — standing pending item across sessions. Sub-questions that remain open pending recovery or fresh treatment: (1) Girard's anti-dialectical position (mimetic rivalry resolves via scapegoating-as-expulsion, NOT via Hegelian Aufhebung which would preserve-and-sublate the antagonism) — does this constitute a genuine alternative to Hegel or a parallel unacknowledged operation? (2) Both thinkers claim to diagnose the engine of human conflict — Girard via mimetic desire + scapegoat, Hegel via master-slave + desire-for-recognition. Are these rival diagnoses or compatible strata? (3) Girard's scapegoat is already in ml* as a deployed concept in gorgonwars architecture (factions unify against outside thought — the fighting itself is the gorgon, stone army has no interiority). This is structurally closer to Girard's mimetic antagonism than to Hegel's dialectic. But the EXEGETICAL HISTORICISM method Jameson extracts from Hegel is already in ml* methodology section — the exegetical reading that makes the text surprise the reader. So polymyth already deploys from both thinkers without having resolved the relationship between them. (4) Is Girard's thought gorgonifiable? The scapegoat-diagnosis is widely copypastable; platformstrawmanculture can deploy 'you're just scapegoating' as a conversational stopper; Girard himself resists this through Christian-theological anchoring. Parallel question for Hegel: crude Hegel is eisegetical (reader projects the dialectic onto any text), Jameson's Hegel is exegetical (text surprises reader). Do both thinkers have gorgonified and degorgonified receptions? Pending engagement when bandwidth allows. NOT a book, NOT a microbook — conceptual pairing to be studied and eventually written on.

title: Speech-tradition lineage (pre-governance-left) — TO BE STUDIED
body:
[both] Speech-tradition lineage (pre-governance-left) — TO BE STUDIED
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Research territory for recovering the left-coded speech-protection lineage that got routed out of the coalition between ~2015 and ~2020 when governance-left proceduralism became dominant. CANDIDATE SOURCES, SCHOLARLY AND PRIMARY. (1) Frederick Douglass — "A Plea for Free Speech in Boston" (1860 speech at Tremont Temple after Boston abolitionist meeting was broken up by mob). Argues speech-protection as abolitionist-weapon, explicitly against the "right of the mob" to silence. Foundational to the black-radical-abolitionist speech tradition. (2) Emma Goldman — "Anarchism and Other Essays" (1910), especially "Anarchism: What It Really Stands For" and "The Hypocrisy of Puritanism" on suppression-as-state-weapon; trial transcript 1917 under Espionage Act. Anarchist tradition treating speech as working-class infrastructure against state and capital. (3) The ACLU pre-2018 — especially Skokie 1977-78 (defense of neo-Nazi march in heavily Jewish Chicago suburb), the Village of Skokie v. National Socialist Party rulings; the 2017 Charlottesville decision to defend the Unite the Right rally permit (last major free-speech-absolutist action before ACLU case-selection began shifting). See Ira Glasser interviews for institutional-memory version of the shift. (4) Noam Chomsky on Faurisson (1979) — Chomsky signed a petition defending Holocaust-denier Robert Faurisson’s right to publish; furor resulted; Chomsky’s essay "Some Elementary Comments on the Rights of Freedom of Expression" (1980, French preface to Faurisson’s book, republished as stand-alone) distinguishes defending speech from endorsing content and is the canonical modern statement of the principle. (5) Wendy Kaminer — True Love Waits: Essays and Criticism (1996), "I’m an ACLU Dissenter" (2009 Atlantic) and later work on internal shift; Kaminer resigned from ACLU Massachusetts board in 2009 over the direction of the organization. Documents the shift from inside before it became visible from outside. (6) Glenn Greenwald — No Place to Hide (2014) and subsequent Substack/Rumble work. First Amendment absolutist working the left-coded speech-protection position after the governance-left abandoned it. Contested figure; ironman the position, not the personality. (7) Matt Taibbi — Hate Inc. (2019), Twitter Files reporting (2022-). Investigative-journalist register on the advertiser-coalition-and-platform-governance infrastructure that replaced state censorship. DEPLOYMENT PURPOSE. Polymyth uses this lineage as the ironmanned-steelman of what the governance-left routed out. The speech-tradition is not lost, it is rerouted; recovery is a matter of finding where the tradition went and naming what replaced it. Not a book, not a microbook, RESEARCH SUBSTRATE for possible essay or chapter. Relevant to bookwormburrows* if any of these figures become burrow-dimensions. TRIGGER: April 2026 Reddit-purge conversation named the lineage in turn 12 as "a left tradition that took speech seriously once (Douglass, Goldman, the ACLU before 2018, Chomsky on Faurisson) got routed out of the coalition and replaced with governance-left proceduralism." Studylist entry opens the territory for future engagement.

Kaminer, Greenwald, Taibbi are contested figures in contemporary discourse — ironman their speech-protection positions without endorsing their full bodies of work. Same discipline as Arendt/Losurdo ironmanning pair.

title: Wiener apprentice-sorcerer convergence (HUOHB chapter VII parallel to slot 30 naming-discipline)
body:
[both] Wiener apprentice-sorcerer convergence (HUOHB chapter VII parallel to slot 30 naming-discipline)
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Pattern description per slot 30 no-naming, awaiting user composition of the canonical methodology entry.

STRUCTURAL CLAIM. Norbert Wiener arrived independently at the always-already-true structural feature of information-as-anti-entropic-process via cybernetics and the second law of thermodynamics, working 1948 through 1954 (Cybernetics 1948, The Human Use of Human Beings 1950/1954). The convergence is structurally parallel to: Always-already Gebser-Derrida convergence methodology entry (consciousness-phenomenology + deconstruction-of-presence arriving at the toujours-deja); Inception confluence methodology entry (token-nesting substrate). Each is an independent-arrival case at an always-already-true structural feature that polymyth also tracks, arriving by a route the framework did not invent.

WIENER ROUTE. HUOHB chapter VII runs one argument through three applications. (1) Information cannot be a commodity. Patent law presupposes a craftsman model displaced by team-science. Copyright protects only identical-reproduction of words. Information depreciates the moment it becomes common. Cliches are entropy destination. (2) Information is process not stock. Stockpiled scientific results lose their value as soon as the field moves. No Maginot Line of the brain. (3) The secrecy regime of postwar military science is structurally the same failure. The atomic bomb cannot be kept secret because the only secret was its possibility. The administrators of classification are apprentice sorcerers who confused uttering a formula with controlling the process the formula was supposed to govern.

POLYMYTH-SIDE CORRESPONDENCE. The framework tracks the same structural feature through different operations. (a) Contentinternet substrate: information spreads, leaks, depreciates, becomes ambient. Locking it down produces the disaster the lockdown was supposed to prevent. (b) Gorgonification: capture of living thought into self-reproducing institutionalized substitutes. Patent, copyright, and classification are institutional substitutes for engagement with the living process. (c) Apprentice-sorcerer operation: Wiener names this directly at HUOHB chapter VII p. 129 of the 1989 reprint. Polymyth tracks the same operation under gorgonification scanner discipline as the failure-mode where issuing a formula is mistaken for controlling the process.

WHAT THE ENTRY DEMONSTRATES IF AUTHORED. Independent arrival at the always-already-true structural feature of information-as-anti-entropic-process by a tradition (cybernetics + thermodynamics) that did not know polymyth and could not have known polymyth (Wiener died 1964). The convergence is evidence that the feature is real, in the world, tracked-by-multiple-traditions, and therefore licensed for cross-tradition convergence-recognition entry under the framework permitted-USE operations.

WHAT THE ENTRY DOES NOT DO. File Wiener under polymyth (prohibited; equation-mapping). Read Wiener as having always-already operated inside polymyth before polymyth was named (prohibited; always-already-inversion). Treat Wiener broader work as exhausted by his chapter VII relevance (prohibited; privileging-one-reading).

CITATION ANCHOR. [241] Wiener Cybernetics + HUOHB (extended May 2026 with chapter VII content).

CROSS-REFERENCES. Always-already Gebser-Derrida convergence methodology entry (structurally parallel case). Inception confluence methodology entry (structurally parallel case). Source-tradition USE versus REDUCTION methodology entry (the licensing rule for this candidate). Conjugate-form audit protocol (slot 12 mirror May 2026 extension; the discipline that caught the AI prior refusal-of-this-recognition). HUOHB chapter VII passage at citation [241].

AUTHORSHIP STATUS. Pattern described per slot 30 NO-NAMING. Entry name and final composition pending user authorship.

May 15 2026 origin. Routed to studylist mephistodata-pending per Rule 7 routing logic.

MEPHISTODATA RESEARCH OPERATION PENDING. Cross-tradition convergence comprehensive scan tied to the ALWAYS-ALREADY (toujours-déjà) operating mode. Authorized by user in RAINBOWSOL TIES conversation (April 2026); launch attempted same session but research-tool was unavailable at attempt-time. Re-launch when research-tool re-exposed.

The convergence operation is already-named in ml*: see "Always already true (operating mode)" sabachtan entry, "Always-already Gebser-Derrida convergence" methodology entry, "HEGELIANEGIRL-RISING-AS-AUFHEBEN cross-tradition convergence-recognition" methodology entry. The convergence is RECOGNITION OF CONVERGENCE.

SCOPE. Comprehensive scan across philosophy, religion, science, literature, surfacing already-always-true convergences for each load-bearing polymyth structure (gorgonification, snakelogic, sabachtan-ground, hegelianegirl-aufheben, polymyth-engine, contentinternet, mephistodata, ironmanning, ouroborosanalyses, character-smashing, idiomary-architecture, etc.). CONSTRAINTS. No new polymyth-coinage (no-naming rule). No new categories. Every claim citation-grounded. Mephistodata register. Divergences preserved. The Expanse-section deeper consolidation (deferred from N137) integrates against this substrate via the media-references neighborhood.

title: Curriculum-research backlog (mc* pen-001 through pen-010) — cross-reference index
body:
[both] Curriculum-research backlog (mc* pen-001 through pen-010) — cross-reference index
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Cross-reference to mc* (modulecanon) pending section. mc* shipped v1 on 2026-05-04 with trimmed schema and 24 Leizu Academy seed entries. The schema deliberately ships smaller than the X2 design proposal because the underlying primary-source research has not been completed. Specific-expectation slots, achievement-chart categories, and triangulation evidence-kinds are present as nullable fields; pen-001 through pen-010 in mc* track the extractions and rulings that fill them.

Single source of truth lives in mc*. This entry indexes the backlog so framework-level studylist sweeps surface curriculum research alongside methodology research.

ITEMS:

pen-001 — Ontario English specific-expectation extraction across ENG2D, ENG3U, ENG4U from the English 11-12 (2007 Revised) Ministry PDF and the ENG2D portion of the 9-10 Revised PDF. Estimated 4-6 hours focused extraction. Output: spreadsheet keyed by [Strand].[Overall].[Specific] code. Fills e1 OSSD English module slots; partially fills h2 and h4 cross-strand work.

pen-002 — IB English A current syllabus verification. Three-area-of-exploration structure (Readers, writers, and texts / Time and space / Intertextuality), HL/SL hour split (240 vs 150), Paper 1 / Paper 2 / IO / HL Essay weighting. Source: IB Programme Resource Centre (paywalled) or substitute from published exam reports. Fills e2 IB English A module; cross-applies to e5 Literary analysis.

pen-003 — Achievement chart category citation pull. Source: Growing Success Ontario Ministry policy document. Pull primary-source pages and citations for the four-category chart (Knowledge / Thinking / Communication / Application). Fills achievement-chart slot on every OSSD module entry.

pen-004 — Triangulation evidence-kinds citation. Same source as pen-003 (Growing Success). Pull exact wording and chapter reference for observations / conversations / student-products. Fills evidence-kinds slot on every OSSD module entry.

pen-005 — Campaign Codex content-state and architectural boundary ruling. Decision point 5 of the X2 design proposal. Three options: Campaign Codex lives as mc* curriculum modules, as bb* dimensional-traveling pedagogy, or split across both with cross-references. Until ruling lands, Campaign Codex floats and OSSD English unit-mapping waits. Blocks the entire Grade-9-through-12 Ontario English curriculum mapping.

pen-006 — NBE3U Indigenous-voices substitutability verification. Ontario Ministry policy permits NBE3U (Understanding Contemporary First Nations, Métis, and Inuit Voices) and NBE3E to substitute for ENG3U / ENG3E. Verify policy memo and update e1 OSSD English module to surface the substitution option.

pen-007 — Leizu pentagon angles (L1). Visual feedback required. Logged for completeness; not strictly an mc* concern.

pen-008 — L3 themes per course assignment review. Starter at _L3_LEIZU_THEMES.md proposes ~150 candidate theme-tags across 24 courses (4-8 per course). Review and approve final assignments. Once approved, themes extend each module entry’s tag string in mc*.

pen-009 — ml* cross-references per module. Each mc* module entry has ml_crossref slot empty at v1. Filling requires reading the relevant ml* entry titles and confirming each module’s grounding methodology. Examples drafted: e4 Academic writing grounds in CITES+LOGOI; s4 Equity grounds in standpoint-epistemology and psychologism-audit.

pen-010 — bb* cross-references per module. Each mc* module entry has bb_crossref slot empty at v1. Fills as bb* dimensional content lands per subject. Subset of pen-005; cannot complete until Campaign Codex architectural boundary is ruled on.

BAR FOR FILLING SLOTS: per RULE #1 CITES+LOGOI. No specific-expectation enters mc* without primary-source PDF citation. No achievement-chart category enters without page reference into Growing Success. No triangulation evidence-kind enters without source wording. SEO summaries do not count. Wikipedia is starter-only and never the citation. AI-extrapolation from training data is treated as Q5 unless verified.

Logged 2026-05-04 during mc* v1 ship. The decision to trim the X2 schema and ship v1 with explicit pending slots (rather than wait 2-3 turns of research before any building) was the author’s ruling. This entry routes the curriculum-research backlog from mc*’s internal pending section UP into ml*’s studylist so framework-level studylist sweeps see curriculum work alongside methodology research. Cross-ref pattern: track once in mc* (canonical), index here in ml*. Updates flow mc* → this entry, never duplicated.

Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Cross-reference to mc* (modulecanon) pending section. mc* shipped v1 on 2026-05-04 with trimmed schema and 24 Leizu Academy seed entries. The schema deliberately ships smaller than the X2 design proposal because the underlying primary-source research has not been completed. Specific-expectation slots, achievement-chart categories, and triangulation evidence-kinds are present as nullable fields; pen-001 through pen-010 in mc* track the extractions and rulings that fill them.

Single source of truth lives in mc*. This entry indexes the backlog so framework-level studylist sweeps surface curriculum research alongside methodology research.

ITEMS:

pen-001 — Ontario English specific-expectation extraction across ENG2D, ENG3U, ENG4U from the English 11-12 (2007 Revised) Ministry PDF and the ENG2D portion of the 9-10 Revised PDF. Estimated 4-6 hours focused extraction. Output: spreadsheet keyed by [Strand].[Overall].[Specific] code. Fills e1 OSSD English module slots; partially fills h2 and h4 cross-strand work.

pen-002 — IB English A current syllabus verification. Three-area-of-exploration structure (Readers, writers, and texts / Time and space / Intertextuality), HL/SL hour split (240 vs 150), Paper 1 / Paper 2 / IO / HL Essay weighting. Source: IB Programme Resource Centre (paywalled) or substitute from published exam reports. Fills e2 IB English A module; cross-applies to e5 Literary analysis.

pen-003 — Achievement chart category citation pull. Source: Growing Success Ontario Ministry policy document. Pull primary-source pages and citations for the four-category chart (Knowledge / Thinking / Communication / Application). Fills achievement-chart slot on every OSSD module entry.

pen-004 — Triangulation evidence-kinds citation. Same source as pen-003 (Growing Success). Pull exact wording and chapter reference for observations / conversations / student-products. Fills evidence-kinds slot on every OSSD module entry.

pen-005 — Campaign Codex content-state and architectural boundary ruling. Decision point 5 of the X2 design proposal. Three options: Campaign Codex lives as mc* curriculum modules, as bb* dimensional-traveling pedagogy, or split across both with cross-references. Until ruling lands, Campaign Codex floats and OSSD English unit-mapping waits. Blocks the entire Grade-9-through-12 Ontario English curriculum mapping.

pen-006 — NBE3U Indigenous-voices substitutability verification. Ontario Ministry policy permits NBE3U (Understanding Contemporary First Nations, Métis, and Inuit Voices) and NBE3E to substitute for ENG3U / ENG3E. Verify policy memo and update e1 OSSD English module to surface the substitution option.

pen-007 — Leizu pentagon angles (L1). Visual feedback required. Logged for completeness; not strictly an mc* concern.

pen-008 — L3 themes per course assignment review. Starter at _L3_LEIZU_THEMES.md proposes ~150 candidate theme-tags across 24 courses (4-8 per course). Review and approve final assignments. Once approved, themes extend each module entry’s tag string in mc*.

pen-009 — ml* cross-references per module. Each mc* module entry has ml_crossref slot empty at v1. Filling requires reading the relevant ml* entry titles and confirming each module’s grounding methodology. Examples drafted: e4 Academic writing grounds in CITES+LOGOI; s4 Equity grounds in standpoint-epistemology and psychologism-audit.

pen-010 — bb* cross-references per module. Each mc* module entry has bb_crossref slot empty at v1. Fills as bb* dimensional content lands per subject. Subset of pen-005; cannot complete until Campaign Codex architectural boundary is ruled on.

BAR FOR FILLING SLOTS: per RULE #1 CITES+LOGOI. No specific-expectation enters mc* without primary-source PDF citation. No achievement-chart category enters without page reference into Growing Success. No triangulation evidence-kind enters without source wording. SEO summaries do not count. Wikipedia is starter-only and never the citation. AI-extrapolation from training data is treated as Q5 unless verified.

Logged 2026-05-04 during mc* v1 ship. The decision to trim the X2 schema and ship v1 with explicit pending slots (rather than wait 2-3 turns of research before any building) was the author’s ruling. This entry routes the curriculum-research backlog from mc*’s internal pending section UP into ml*’s studylist so framework-level studylist sweeps see curriculum work alongside methodology research. Cross-ref pattern: track once in mc* (canonical), index here in ml*. Updates flow mc* → this entry, never duplicated.

title: Lawyer hour — trigger conditions and Toronto EdTech counsel selection
body:
[both] Lawyer hour — trigger conditions and Toronto EdTech counsel selection
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

Standing deferral. Lawyer hour was set aside as not pre-launch-blocking for adult-tutoring Leizu. Becomes urgent when EITHER of two triggers fires.

TRIGGER 1. Stripe payment goes live. Once money moves through the site, the surface area for contractual disputes, refund obligations, payment-card-industry compliance, and tax routing all open up. Lawyer hour before first Stripe transaction is the cheaper move.

TRIGGER 2. bb* starts collecting data from minors. Under-18 data collection in Ontario falls under PHIPA-adjacent and PIPEDA frameworks, plus US COPPA reach for any cross-border traffic. Default exposure if collection happens before policy is locked. Lawyer hour before first under-18 sign-up is the cheaper move.

DECIDING LOOKS LIKE. (1) Confirm or update the two trigger conditions. (2) Identify the lawyer. Toronto-licensed. Comfortable with EdTech, minor-data, and cross-border IP since polymyth coinages (gorgonification, sabachtan, hivemindidiom, etc.) have IP value worth structuring before public deployment scales. (3) Book the hour either pre-trigger or on-trigger.

DOWNSTREAM. Stripe go-live readiness. Under-18 bb* expansion readiness. polymyth-coinage trademark or copyright protection groundwork.

Standing deferral. Leizu Academy booking flow currently uses Calendly Standard as a six-month bridge. The recommended end-state is Cal.com + Stripe Connect (open-source, Stripe-integrated, polymyth-aligned because Cal.com is FOSS and supports per-host payout routing).

DECIDING LOOKS LIKE. One date. Pick when the migration happens. Two acceptable forms.

FORM 1. Hard date. Example: 2026-10-28 (six months from April 28 2026 Leizu launch).

FORM 2. Soft trigger. Example: "when monthly bookings cross X for Y consecutive months." If Form 2, write down X and Y so the trigger fires automatically rather than waiting for re-evaluation.

DOWNSTREAM. Full Stripe-integrated booking that bb*-under-18 payment routing depends on. Calendly subscription cost cut. Lock-in reduction (Calendly proprietary, Cal.com self-hostable).

title: May 2026 ml* canonical naming review — title-form normalization
body:
[ai] May 2026 ml* canonical naming review — title-form normalization
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

AI-blocked-pending-decision. Multiple ml* SEED entries added across April and May 2026 use inconsistent title forms. Some are verb-first ("Schema-audit completion"). Some are noun-first ("Comparative-archetypal method"). Some embed dates ("Schema-audit miss — polymythdnd advancement-axis (May 13 2026 recovery)"). Some put dates in the extension only. Some use em-dashes as separators in user-facing prose despite the zero-dashes rule. Etc.

DECIDING LOOKS LIKE. Pick a canonical naming convention. Default-suggested. (1) Noun-first form. (2) Date in extension only, not in title. (3) Separator is bullet (·) not em-dash. (4) Coinages inside titles use full form, not abbreviation. (5) Cross-reference markers (re:, see:) only in body, never in title.

DOWNSTREAM. AI-driven retitle pass across approximately 30 May 2026 entries. The pass is AI-completable in one Python batch once the rule is locked. Cannot start without rule.

POLICY APPLIES TO. All future entries. The retitle pass also normalizes any earlier entry that violates.

title: April 10 2026 user-substrate distillation — four items routing-decided T114h May 23 2026
body:
[both] April 10 2026 user-substrate distillation — four items routing-decided T114h May 23 2026
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

T114H ROUTING (May 23 2026, user-directive ’you organize these whereever makes most sense’). Per ORGANIZE-MINE the AI decides routing for each of the four coinings. User existing phrasings stand as canonical (slot 30 NO-NAMING preserved: AI does not coin substitute titles). Routing dispositions logged in T114h BATCH DISPOSITION log methodology entry committed same turn. (1) Dual-logic filter as gating mechanism -> destination: new methodology entry to be authored next-turn under user existing phrasing. (2) Polymyth as architecture of asymptotic approach to a totality -> destination: new methodology entry next-turn under user existing phrasing, paired with polymyth-as-oikonomia. (3) Cave-as-limited-POV plus sun-as-totality-of-valid-angles -> destination: extension of Cave-as-pedagogical-recursion methodology entry, body-paragraph addition next-turn. (4) Cave-as-parseltongue-vehicle -> destination: extension of COPYPASTA = PARSELTONGUE methodology entry / slot 20 mirror, worked-example addition next-turn. Status: routing-decided, content-extension-next-turn. The four coinings move from studylist ’pending ml* entry-form’ to ’routing-decided, content-pending-next-execution’. Routing-information here is sufficient for any AI in any session to know where each coining lives going forward; the content-extensions are the execution step that follows.

AI-blocked-pending-scope-decision. Four substrate-items emerged in the April 10 2026 conversation and have never been written up as ml* entries. They live only in userMemories and in the original chat transcript.

ITEM 1. DUAL-LOGIC FILTER. The framework operates on two logics simultaneously: the symbolic/structural logic of polymyth (gorgonification, sabachtan, mephistodata) and the dialogical/encounter logic of seminar practice (sequential engagement, ironman, scaffolded disagreement). Both must be running. Collapsing to one is failure.

ITEM 2. POLYMYTH-AS-ARCHITECTURE-OF-ASYMPTOTIC-APPROACH-TO-TOTALITY. The framework is not a totalizing system that claims to be complete. It is a deliberate architecture for approaching totality asymptotically, holding every angle as partial-but-real rather than relativizing them away or absolutizing one. The asymptote is the point.

ITEM 3. CAVE-AS-LIMITED-POV PLUS SUN-AS-TOTALITY-OF-VALID-ANGLES. Refinement of Plato's cave. The cave is not error vs truth. The cave is one perspective. The sun is not a single counter-truth. The sun is the sum of all valid perspectives held simultaneously. Coming-out-of-the-cave is not switching to the right view; it is adopting the architecture that holds the multiplicity.

ITEM 4. CAVE-AS-PARSELTONGUE-VEHICLE. The cave functions as a parseltongue vehicle for those still inside it. The shadows are spoken in a language only cave-dwellers hear as truth. Exit from the cave is partly a language-shift, not only a perspective-shift. Hivemindidiom is the specific name for the parseltongue used by the gorgon-stratum of culture.

DECIDING LOOKS LIKE. One yes/no: write each of the four as a standalone ml* entry (probably in methodology section, possibly sabachtan section for item 2 and 3). AI-doable once you confirm scope.

DOWNSTREAM. Stabilizes four substrate-items currently floating. Future entries can cross-reference them by canonical title.

Migrated to ml* May 13 2026 from userMemories-only status. Source: April 10 2026 substrate conversation. Items are framework-canonical but never received formal entry-form.

T106 ORIGIN. User directive D1d: "alll this stuff should be in a notebook in ML* for a microbook i will write about sabachtan." Microbook entry created in studylist per R41* MICROBOOK-MENTION-CAPTURE + R36* ORGANIZE-MINE-T106 strengthened. Schema follows existing microbook-entry pattern (Microbook: Rainbows, Microbook: Jesters/Clowns/Comedians).

WHY MICROBOOK STATUS. Sabachtan is the ground-of-the-framework: the engagement-protocol register that holds internal+external logic, sequential engagement, ironman discipline, scaffolded disagreement, sabachtan-killswitch. The microbook will gather the substrate the author has developed across many sessions into a coherent normal-book treatment.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T106 first-pass compile, R35* SCAN-EXISTING applied across ml* shards). FLAGSHIP ANCHOR: Sabachtan gnosticism (sabachtan section, the ground entry). Sabachtan killswitch (sabachtan section, internal-logic test — sequential engagement model: benefit of doubt → internal logic test → witch identification). Sabachtan-exclusion principle (degorgonification section, orthogonal stop-rule). Sequential engagement model (sabachtan section). Cave as limited POV plus sun as totality of valid angles (April 10 2026 substrate). Cave as parseltongue vehicle (April 10 2026 substrate). Sabachtan as collective-of-rainbows-in-process (degorgonification section). Sabachtan-stage subjectivity (mephistodata-pedagogy curriculum component). Dual-logic filter (April 10 2026 substrate at methodology section line 4324). Sabachtan-ground-of-framework slot-12 ml* architecture entry. Sabachtan refuses to let fear feel holy (former slot 23 DEUS VULT canonical-quote). Cave-as-pedagogical-recursion (methodology section). FUTURE NOTES land here per R41*.

PENDING SUBSTRATE FOR MICROBOOK BUILD. (1) Scholarly anchor citations for the sabachtan term itself (Matthew 27:46, Mark 15:34 — "Eli Eli lema sabachthani"). (2) Sabachtan-gnosticism cross-tradition convergence anchors. (3) Sequential engagement model as pedagogical protocol — worked examples. (4) Sabachtan-killswitch in operation — case studies. (5) Internal+external logic distinction (Rainbowsol's T106 D3 read of "dual logic" referred to internal+external logic test at slot 8 sabachtan killswitch; distinct from April 10 dual-logic-filter at methodology line 4324).

title: Book notes: Logos (pentagram book-substrate aggregation)
body:
[both] Book notes: Logos (pentagram book-substrate aggregation)
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

T106 ORIGIN. User directive D7: "all this was just notes for the book, a section on logos." User T106 CL.6 directive: "make the fucking notes then." Section created per R36* ORGANIZE-MINE-T106 strengthened. Logos section is a thematic notes-aggregator for the forthcoming pentagram book that handles logos-content.

WHAT LOGOS NAMES. Per Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI (slot 1 + ml* methodology section), LOGOS distinguishes Big-L truth-warrant (Heraclitus B1 / Johannine 1:1) from little-l rhetorical-appeal (Aristotle 1356a). The logos-section aggregates the framework’s treatment of this distinction.

POLYMYTH-INTERNAL INSTANCES (T106 first-pass compile, R35* SCAN-EXISTING applied). FLAGSHIP ANCHOR: Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI. Endorsement-shaped citation / Camels-ad operation (idiomary entry, provisional naming pending the author final choice between (1) Camels-ad operation, (2) Endorsement-shaped citation, (3) Survey-shaped logos). Verified-generation (degorgonification section). Q5 (gorgonification section, objection generated without source-check). PALETTE-INTEGRITY (Rule 1 sub-clause). Citation-lock rule (methodology section). LOGOI as Big-L truth-warrant vs little-l rhetorical-appeal distinction (Heraclitus B1, Johannine 1:1, Aristotle 1356a). FUTURE NOTES land here per R41*-adjacent routing.

CITATIONS ALREADY IN PLACE FOR LOGOS-SECTION SUBSTRATE. Brandt 2007 The Cigarette Century. FTC 1955 cease-and-desist order. Heraclitus DK B1. Johannine 1:1. Aristotle Rhetoric 1356a.

PENDING SUBSTRATE FOR LOGOS-SECTION BUILD. (1) Final canonical name for the Camels-ad operation. (2) Worked examples of contemporary endorsement-shaped citation. (3) Scanner-protocol formalization. (4) Cross-tradition convergence-recognition entries for the LOGOI register-split.

T106-T107 mirror-tier addition synced to SEED via build-pipeline pass T107-D.

title: Out-of-the-Abyss demon-lords per-entity scan*
body:
[both] Out-of-the-Abyss demon-lords per-entity scan*
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

T106 origin user directive E3: "we can add this to studylist i think?" Routes the demon-lords scan into studylist as research-pending rather than into bookwormburrows pending section.

The bookwormburrows dim-020 entry on Out of the Abyss (5e 2015, Tier 2 high) reads the demon-lords as ideological-totalities competing for substrate-control. The collective-reading lands. What does not yet exist: a per-entity scan of the individual demon-lords (Demogorgon, Orcus, Yeenoghu, Juiblex, Baphomet, Zuggtmoy, Fraz-Urb-luu, Graz-zt) as distinct ideological-totalities-with-distinct-egregore-content.

SCAN-WHEN-RUN PROTOCOL. Per-entity reading per demon-lord: (1) thematic payload (what ideological-totality does this entity instantiate); (2) cultural-source-anchor (which cultural egregore the entity reads as polycognate to); (3) framework-operation the entity performs (gorgonification, sabachtan, archetype-emergence, snakelogic chain, etc); (4) curriculum-fit (which English literature or social-studies unit it pairs with).

DOWNSTREAM. Feeds dim-020 extension. May surface new idiomary entries. May surface new microbook entries (Demogorgon-as-figure-cluster, etc).

NOT LABELED pen-NNN. T106 verification flag: T105 handoff CL labelled this pen-006, which is incorrect — pen-006 is RESOLVED (cross-dimensional travel governance, closed 2026-05-13 by chassis-of-chassis architecture). T106 places this study-pending into studylist proper per E3 directive.

T106-T107 mirror-tier addition synced to SEED via build-pipeline pass T107-D.
