title: T107 RESIDUE — entries resisting cross-catalogue synthesis (Rainbowsol personal-decision list)
body:
[both] T107 RESIDUE — entries resisting cross-catalogue synthesis (Rainbowsol personal-decision list)
LAYMAN. Study-list entry. The body below specifies the reading/research item, its substrate-relevance to the framework, and its register (author-pending if user-authorship-required, Mephistodata-pending if AI-executable). Tracks pending research/reading work — not yet integrated into ml*/mc*/bb*/cc* substrates.

T107 ORIGIN. Synthesis pass over the ~260-entry T106 TTRPG catalogue executed at 2026-05-21 in two installments (T107-A operations 1-12 + T107-B operations 13-20 plus residue plus tail). Twenty polymyth-operations identified with multiple independent witness systems each. Residue below is the set of entries whose distinctive structural feature appears in only one or two catalogue items OR whose contribution does not cluster across the catalogue at the operation-layer.

RESIDUE ENTRIES.

Tékumel (M.A.R. Barker 1975 onward). Linguistic-Mesoamerican world built across decades by single creator who constructed actual languages (Tsolyáni) and cultures. Sui generis at linguistic-cosmology layer. Candidate for microbook on creator-as-linguistic-cosmologist (Tolkien-substrate-adjacent).

Skyrealms of Jorune (Andrew Leker, Miles Teves 1985 onward). Alien-creature-bestiary world where design-substrate IS alien-biology and alien-language of the iscin-species. Candidate for companion-species/alien-biology microbook research.

Talislanta (Steve Sechi 1987). "No Elves, No Dwarves, No Halflings" design-by-negation philosophy. Case for microbook on design-by-negation as creative-discipline.

Castle Falkenstein (Mike Pondsmith 1994). Diceless steampunk Faerie-court-salon. Card-based resolution. Specific design-philosophy of letter-and-salon-and-card is sui generis.

Bushido (Bob Charrette + Paul Hume 1979). Early Japanese-feudal RPG. Historically significant as one of first published RPGs with deep cultural-anthropological-research substrate outside Anglo-Western settings.

Tunnels and Trolls (Ken St. Andre 1975). Second-published-RPG-ever after D&D. Distinctive for inventing the solo-mode (solitaire-adventures from launch). Ancestor-witness for OP-9 solo-as-authorship.

Stormbringer / Elric. Moorcock licensed setting on BRP-substrate. Residue for licensed-property-as-substrate single-witness cluster.

Rolemaster / MERP. Chart-driven mechanical-complexity as design-substrate.

Hollow Earth Expedition. Ubiquity dice system — unique mechanical-substrate. Single-system mechanical-innovation.

Würm (French paleolithic). Cluster-partial under OP-10 + OP-16; distinctive for non-modern-language-design-discipline.

Tagmar (Brazilian) and 3D&T (Brazilian). Residue pending deeper research on Brazilian-historical-substrate engagement.

Pichi the Pink Fairy Armadillo, Amargo y retruco, Carpinchos en guerra (capybaras at war Spanish edition), Barras del Espacio (Argentinian itch.io entries from T106 D.1 gap-fill). Cultural-substrate features but catalogue-notes too thin for operation-layer this turn.

Earth Mother Sky Father (Morgan Davie 7QRPG). DOUBLE-PLACED in OP-17 LANGUAGE-AS-MECHANIC and residue for indigenous-cosmology-specificity (Māori atua creation-story not just language but cosmological-substrate).

Tipua: The Wrath of Ngārara (4Phase Aotearoa). Cluster-partial OP-10 + OP-16. Residue pending deeper research.

Macuahuitl (OSR pre-Columbian Mexico). DOUBLE-PLACED under OP-12 colonization-as-substrate and residue.

KiwiRPG umbrella organization. Not a game itself; organization-as-substrate. Residue: single-witness cluster.

Sword World, Shinobigami, Double Cross (Japanese TTRPGs). Cluster-partial OP-15 + OP-10. Residue-flag for design-philosophy-of-Japanese-TTRPG-tradition microbook research. Undersourced at substantive-design-philosophy level.

FLAGGED-GAPS (not residue — absences in the catalogue itself):

Indigenous Australian native-designed TTRPGs. ABSENT from catalogue per T106 D.2 caveat. bb*’s anti-colonial substrate incomplete without indigenous-Australian-native-designed-work.

Native-Arab-designer TTRPGs. Arab-world entries surfaced T106 D.3 are predominantly outsider-designed (French Capharnaum, Anglophone Cairo Guidebook).

MICROBOOK CANDIDATES from residue. Tékumel for creator-as-linguistic-cosmologist microbook. Skyrealms of Jorune for companion-species/alien-biology microbook. Talislanta for design-by-negation microbook. Japanese-TTRPG tradition (Sword World/Shinobigami/Double Cross/Bushido) for cross-cultural-design-philosophy microbook.

T106-T107 mirror-tier addition synced to SEED via build-pipeline pass T107-D.

title: Microbook proposals (T113 May 22 2026, four candidates from T107 TTRPG residue)
body:
[both] Microbook proposals (T113 May 22 2026, four candidates from T107 TTRPG residue)
LAYMAN. New studylist section collecting future-microbook proposals. Each proposal names a candidate microbook research-thread surfaced by the T107 polymyth-method synthesis pass over the 260-game tabletop-roleplay catalogue. The four games below sit outside the 20-operation clusters T107 identified but each suggests its own microbook research-thread. Section created per Rainbowsol's directive T113 May 22 2026: “add these to a new section called microbook proposals.”

CANDIDATE 1. Tekumel microbook (M.A.R. Barker, 1975 onward). Topic: creator-as-linguistic-cosmologist. One creator built actual working languages (Tsolyani, Yan Koryani) over four-plus decades. Sui generis case at the linguistic-cosmology layer. Substrate for understanding how creator-built-language operates as world-substrate. Tolkien-adjacent but distinct because Barker’s project ran across novels, the Empire of the Petal Throne tabletop-roleplay system, and linguistic-anthropological depth (Barker was a professor of South Asian Studies who studied Urdu and other languages professionally).

CANDIDATE 2. Skyrealms of Jorune microbook (Andrew Leker and Miles Teves, 1985 onward). Topic: companion-species and alien-biology as design-substrate. The Iscin-races (Jorune’s seven sentient alien races each genetically engineered from Earth animal stock by the Iscin scientists) operate as biology-first creatures whose ecology drives the entire game-design. Substrate for understanding alien-biology-as-substrate operations across speculative fiction.

CANDIDATE 3. Talislanta microbook (Steve Sechi, 1987 onward). Topic: design-by-negation as creative discipline. The advertising tagline “No Elves, No Dwarves, No Halflings” operates as design-philosophy: the world is built by what is refused. Substrate for understanding negation-as-creation operations across creative work.

CANDIDATE 4. Japanese tabletop-roleplay tradition microbook (Sword World, Shinobigami, Double Cross, Bushido as primary anchors). Topic: cross-cultural design-philosophy. Japanese tabletop tradition operates with distinct conventions across character-creation, mechanic-design, and scenario-architecture compared to the Anglo-Western lineage. Substrate for understanding cross-cultural-design-philosophy operations.

PROPOSAL STATUS. Each candidate sits as RESEARCH-PENDING. Substrate identified, primary anchor named, research not yet started. Each proposal awaits your authorization to begin substantive research.

CROSS-REFERENCES. Existing microbook entries in studylist (sabachtan, rainbows, jesters/clowns/comedians). T107 polymyth-method synthesis (the source of these four proposals; T107-A operations 1-12, T107-B operations 13-20 plus residue, T107-C per-entry scan-tags). T107 residue aggregator entry (the prior holding-location for these candidates before this dedicated section).

T113 May 22 2026 microbook-proposals section creation per Rainbowsol's directive. Substrate from T107 polymyth-method synthesis residue (T107-A operations 1-12 + T107-B operations 13-20 + T107-C per-entry scan-tags + T107 residue aggregator).

title: T114f studylist caveat-tracking (Costikyan source, Hindmarch essay, radiojamesgames URL; items 13/14/16 of T114e bloom)
body:
[ai] T114f studylist caveat-tracking (Costikyan source, Hindmarch essay, radiojamesgames URL; items 13/14/16 of T114e bloom)
LAYMAN. T114f studylist caveat-tracking entry. Three open caveats on Group G citations ratified T106 now have a dedicated studylist anchor so they remain visible without re-bloom. Each caveat awaits user-confirmation at the moment the citation is actually deployed in a future entry. None blocks any current work.

CAVEAT cit-045 (Costikyan). Source ratified T106: I Have No Words and I Must Design (Greg Costikyan 1994; expansion 2002 in Game On anthology). User T29 mentioned a separate piece Where Stories End and Games Begin which AI could not re-verify (specific title/venue/year unresolved). At use-time the operator confirms which Costikyan source is intended for the deploying entry. Until then cit-045 stays bound to I Have No Words and I Must Design.

CAVEAT cit-046 (Hindmarch). Source ratified T106: Will Hindmarch essay within Second Person (ed. Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin, MIT Press 2007, ISBN 978-0-262-08356-0). Hindmarch contributed multiple pieces to the anthology; specific essay-title pending user identification. At use-time the user names the specific essay; the citation entry gets the title appended.

CAVEAT cit-050 (radiojamesgames). Source ratified T106: TTRPGs Need Genres January 2026. Publication URL and full author byline behind the handle radiojamesgames pending verification. At use-time the user supplies URL plus byline; the citation entry gets the metadata appended.

WHY STUDYLIST NOT CITATIONLIST. The citation entries themselves are ratified and live in the citation section (cit-045, cit-046, cit-050). What is tracked here is the open question on each entry, not the entry itself. The studylist is the canonical home for open questions whose resolution requires user-substrate-input. User-directive T114f routed items 13, 14, 16 to studylist explicitly.

CROSS-REFERENCES. Citation section cit-045 Costikyan, cit-046 Second Person, cit-050 radiojamesgames. Pending-user-authorship registry Group G (where the caveats were originally logged at T66 AI-PROPOSED and T106 RATIFIED). T114f BATCH RATIFICATION log methodology entry items 13/14/16.

T114f studylist caveat-tracking for three Group G citations (cit-045 Costikyan source-confirmation, cit-046 Hindmarch essay-identification, cit-050 radiojamesgames URL/byline-verification). Citations themselves ratified; open caveats parked in studylist per user T114f routing.

title: T114f future-CL carve (80-modules-grounding-research + Chinese-rendering + Pentagram-1235 + WotC-into-TTRPG-synthesis)
body:
[both] T114f future-CL carve (80-modules-grounding-research + Chinese-rendering + Pentagram-1235 + WotC-into-TTRPG-synthesis)
LAYMAN. T114f future-CL carve. Four items routed to FUTURE-CL per user-directive T114f: they are NOT in the current CL, NOT in future bloomed CL until execution-turn is invoked, and the AI does not re-surface them. The carve preserves their substrate so when the execution-turn comes the work has a defined starting point.

FUTURE-CL ENTRY 1. Eighty curriculum modules with empty grounding fields (item 3 of T114e bloom). User-directive we will need to add a CL to do the full research to fill the content for this. Scope: 80 modules in modulecanon currently have empty grounding fields (the section linking each module to foundational sources/anchors). Future-CL deliverable: a grounding-research pass that for each module either (a) supplies grounding from existing substrate (Ontario OSSD curriculum codes, IB DP, AP, Cambridge) or (b) flags the module as not-yet-grounded with a research-anchor. Execution turn: when user invokes. Dependencies: mc* current state already provides 80-module substrate; no further upload needed.

FUTURE-CL ENTRY 2. Traditional Chinese rendering of Sabachtan (item 5 of T114e bloom). User-directive i already said, pause this until we are done everything, its a future endevor, never have this in cl anymore, move this to FUTURE CL. Scope: render coined framework terms into Traditional Chinese, starting with Sabachtan. Approach options remain open: (a) find existing Chinese term naming this exact concept; (b) note Chinese names the concept differently; (c) note Chinese does not have this concept; (d) coin a new Chinese compound. Execution turn: user invokes after everything-else-done.

FUTURE-CL ENTRY 3. Pentagram book titles 1, 2, 3, 5 (item 6 of T114e bloom). User-directive move this to FUTURE CL. Scope: user creative authorship of four book titles. Book 4 working title GORGONWARS ratified T114e. Books 1, 2, 3, 5 retain user authorship per PUA Group F discipline. Execution turn: user authors.

FUTURE-CL ENTRY 4. TTRPG-research-synthesis CL absorbing WotC 2021 media-canon doctrine (item 9 of T114e bloom). User-directive we can just add this to the full TTRPG research synthesis we must do soon. Scope: a full TTRPG-as-framework-cognate research synthesis. Will absorb WotC 2021 doctrine (declaring D and D novels, video games, films, live-play sessions non-canonical to the tabletop game). Other queued substrate likely folds in: Costikyan Hindmarch Sanderson radiojamesgames citations, library-of-mediums Sanderson methodology, unnameability-as-degorgonifying-signature (TTRPG canonical case), polymythdnd current build. Execution turn: user invokes TTRPG synthesis.

DISCIPLINE. AI does NOT re-surface any of these four items in any subsequent bloom or CL until user invokes the execution turn. User retains override authority. The carve is the substrate-preservation; the execution-turn is the triggering operation.

CROSS-REFERENCES. T114f BATCH RATIFICATION log methodology entry. Modulecanon mc*. Sabachtan methodology entry. Pentagram book methodology entries. WotC 2021 media-canon doctrine pending entry. Library-of-mediums rule pending-user-authorship entry. Costikyan/Hindmarch/Sanderson/radiojamesgames citation entries. Unnameability-as-natural-degorgonifying-signature methodology entry.

title: AI research list (Mephistodata-pending research-anchors aggregated for execution-turn invocation)
body:
[ai] AI research list (Mephistodata-pending research-anchors aggregated for execution-turn invocation)
LAYMAN. T114h studylist entry. Aggregates AI-doable research items currently awaiting execution-turn invocation. None blocks current work; each surfaces here so the substrate is visible without re-bloom. When user invokes a specific item, AI executes the research-pass and routes findings to the appropriate destination per Audit-every-conversation standing directive.

CURRENT ENTRIES.

RES-1. mc* Pass B English concept-cell build. Fill concept-cells under each of the 76 modules in modulecanon English-curriculum scaffold (F/I/A/J/S tiers; Ontario OSSD plus international equivalents). Cell-substrate sources: existing T67-T69 module-substrate plus ml* Pass A operations. Execution-turn trigger: user invokes ’mc* Pass B execute’ or names which tier or cell-cluster to start. Estimated scope: large multi-turn (cells are numerous; per-cell scope is small but cumulative).

RES-2. Jester microbook source-verification pass. Verify the six anchor sources (Beatrice K. Otto Fools Are Everywhere; Mooney Pueblo sacred-clown ethnography; Ortiz Tewa World plus New Perspectives on the Pueblos; Welsford The Fool; Billington A Social History of the Fool; Hyde Trickster Makes This World; Hynes/Doty Mythical Trickster Figures) per Rule#1 citation discipline, draft microbook outline, surface for user review. Execution-turn trigger: user invokes ’jester microbook execute’. Notes already in jester microbook entry (item 9 of T114g bloom).

RES-3. T105 F1 Harlem Unbound Smithsonian/Met collection verification. Verify the Harlem Unbound second-edition claim (currently sourced only to publisher press release) against Smithsonian Anacostia Community Museum + Met Museum collection records. Execution-turn trigger: user invokes ’F1 verify’ or AI does at next deployment-audit.

RES-4. T105 F2 Coyote and Crow Nebula Award nomination verification. Verify the Coyote and Crow Game Writing Nebula nomination against Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers of America record. Execution-turn trigger: same as RES-3.

RES-5. T105 F3 Torg Eternity Core Earth Sourcebook 2025 release status. Verify against Ulisses Spiele storefront. Execution-turn trigger: same as RES-3.

RES-6. T105 F5 Memory slot capacity audit. Audit which of the 30 memory slots are approaching the 100,000-character cap (PM10 ceiling) and need offloading to ml* coreplus. Execution-turn trigger: user invokes ’memory audit’ or AI does at session-start when memory edits are pending.

RES-7. T105 F6 _redirects file three-URL routing audit. Verify Netlify _redirects file correctly routes /polymyth/bookwormburrows/, /bookwormcard/, /bb/ at next deployment.

RES-8. T107 b-option per-entry scan-tag pass. Per-entry scan-tag pass over the ~260-entry T106 catalogue to confirm each lands in at least one of the 20 T107 polymyth-operations or in the residue list. Currently family-membership stands for individual-entry-cluster-assignment in some cases. A deeper scan would tag every entry individually. Execution-turn trigger: user invokes ’T107 per-entry pass execute’ or AI does as part of T107 -> bb* translation work.

ADDITION PROTOCOL. New AI-doable research-items surface here per Audit-every-conversation standing directive. When a new RES-N is added, the source-conversation reference is preserved so the research-context is recoverable.

CROSS-REFERENCES. Audit-every-conversation standing directive methodology entry (the rule this list operationalizes). ORGANIZE-MINE methodology entry. Modulecanon mc* (RES-1 substrate). Microbook: Jesters/Clowns/Comedians studylist entry (RES-2 destination). T107 polymyth-method synthesis 20 operations methodology entries (RES-8 source). T114h BATCH DISPOSITION log methodology entry (origin record).

T114h studylist entry aggregating Mephistodata-pending AI-doable research items. Currently eight entries (mc* Pass B, jester microbook, F1/F2/F3/F5/F6 from T105 handoff, T107 per-entry pass). Standing addition-protocol per Audit-every-conversation directive.
  tags: studylist, ai-research-list, mephistodata-pending, aggregated-research-items, t114h, may-2026-23, execution-turn-trigger, audit-every-conversation-anchor, dual-audience-schema

title: BB* / PolymythDND clarification and next build queue (2026-06-30)
body:
[both] BB* / PolymythDND clarification and next build queue (2026-06-30)
LAYMAN. Studylist and CL capture from the 2026-06-30 BB clarification pass. BB, BookwormBurrows, and PolymythDND name the same game in different registers. The game is a teacher-led academic TTRPG. The teacher is the Dimensional Master. AI helps behind the curtain with bureaucracy, continuity, setupnpcs, improvnpcs, time, place, quest ledgers, and table memory. Assignments are quests only when they give players in-game motivation to study, write, research, revise, return, or build something that matters inside the dimension. The sealed exegesis stays protected. The game extrapolates from the text rather than rewriting the text.

CURRENT TO DO.
1. Audit the Thank You M'am cmp-001 assignment set and confirm each assignment has a clear in-game motive.
2. Separate the bb* archive into practical use layers without deleting entries: teacher manual, AI conduct doctrine, player-facing explanation, mechanics reference, campaign-building rules, design ledger, and unresolved questions.
3. Update campaign templates to mark setupnpcs and improvnpcs as separate NPC registers.
4. Build a DM table assistant after the BB grammar is stable. It should start by asking for the wormcards, then the anchor text or campaign, and then help the teacher track quests, NPCs, locations, time, items, scars, consequences, and continuity.
5. Add adult seminar mode prompts so university, graduate, or PhD-level groups have game reasons to study independently between sessions.
6. Keep the website as support for table play, not a replacement for the Dimensional Master.

REGISTER. Mixed Rainbowsol and Mephistodata. User rulings are installed. AI-executable cleanup and guard work can proceed. Grammar-changing decisions remain user-involved.

CROSS-REFERENCES. bb* dm-108 naming alias; dm-109 assignments-as-quests motivation rule; dm-110 setupnpc/improvnpc taxonomy; ses-006 adult seminar mode; pen-015 BB* next build list; cmp-001 Thank You M'am campaign; cc* NPC schedule.
